violations.text: Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview pasta was placed in the walk-in cooler in a container greater than four inches deep. Temperature observed to be 49 degrees F. Pasta had been made less than 2 hours prior to inspection and was placed in a shallow pan to complete cooling process. Proper cooling methods reviewed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025

The entrance door is not tight-fitting. Space observed at the bottom of the door. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025
Refrigerator door gasket of the walk-in cooler observed in poor repair, split away from the door. Gasket is to be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the condenser fan guards in the walk-in cooler have an accumulation of dust, dirt, and other debris. Fan guards are to be cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025
